verificationNote: Sourced from individual Ubisoft official operator pages (game-info/operators/<slug>), one fetch per operator, 2026-07-02. Second pass: 24 additional operators (Amaru, Buck, Capitão, Castle, Clash, Doc, Dokkaebi, Echo, Fenrir, Fuse, Gridlock, Jäger, Lion, Maestro, Monty, Nokk, Nomad, Sens, Sledge, Solid Snake, Solis, Vigil, Wamai, Warden) verified via individual Ubisoft official operator pages (game-info/operators/<slug>), 2026-07-02. Note: Ubisoft's URL slugs for Fuse and Monty are 'fuze' and 'montagne' respectively.

Bandit.description: Electrifies surfaces like reinforced walls, deployable shields, and barbed wire to damage attackers and destroy their gadgets.
Bandit.usageNote: Best placed on reinforced walls this operator is personally covering, especially likely hard-breach points.

Valkyrie.description: A deployable camera encased in a tinted silicone ball with micro-suction cups that Valkyrie can place on almost any surface, transmitting live stabilized video to all team operators.
Valkyrie.usageNote: Place on flanks and common attacker approach routes to extend the defending team's intel coverage beyond normal drone cams.

Azami.description: A modified kunai that adheres to surfaces and deploys an expandable material that solidifies into a bulletproof barrier for defensive reinforcement.
Azami.usageNote: Use to reinforce soft walls or block sightlines/vaults where a full hard reinforcement isn't available or needed.

Frost.description: A concealed trap with metal jaws that deploy at ankle and shin height when triggered, immobilizing operators who step on it.
Frost.usageNote: Hide near common attacker entry points such as windows or door frames where attackers are likely to land or dash through.

Smoke.description: Remotely-detonated gas grenades that can be triggered on command to create toxic clouds for area denial and defense.
Smoke.usageNote: Place in doorways or chokepoints leading to the objective so it can be detonated on demand as attackers push through.

Kaid.description: Electrifies reinforced walls, hatches, barbed wire, and deployable shields within a 0.75 meter radius to prevent enemy breaches.
Kaid.usageNote: Attach to reinforced walls or hatches near the objective to deny common hard-breach tools alongside standard reinforcements.

Aruni.description: Projects a laser array that detects movement through it and increases energy output to cause burns upon detection.
Aruni.usageNote: Place across reinforced openings or hatches to punish attackers attempting to pass through that point.

Lesion.description: Mines that inject toxin into opponents who trigger them, injuring and limiting their speed.
Lesion.usageNote: Hide in likely attacker pathways near the objective so triggering one reveals the attacker's position over time.

Alibi.description: Projects holograms that look identical to Alibi; when attackers shoot or touch them, they become marked and pinged for several seconds.
Alibi.usageNote: Place in flank routes or ambiguous approach paths to bait attackers into revealing their position.

Mute.description: Disrupts electronic signals and communications in its vicinity, preventing enemy drones and gadgets from functioning properly nearby.
Mute.usageNote: Place near windows, doors, or reinforced walls to jam attacker drones and stop electronic breaching tools before they can be used.

Ela.description: Sticky concussion mines that trigger on proximity, impairing hearing and causing a dizzying effect to anyone within the blast radius.
Ela.usageNote: Place near likely attacker entry points or flanks to disorient pushes before they reach the objective.

Kapkan.description: A trap that deploys a motion-activated C4 charge, placed on door and window frames to deny entry points to attackers.
Kapkan.usageNote: Place on door or window frames along likely attacker entry routes rather than on the objective's main reinforced approach.

Melusi.description: A bulletproof device that deploys on surfaces and automatically releases a disorienting scream when attackers approach, slowing their movement.
Melusi.usageNote: Place near common attacker approach lanes into the objective to slow pushes and buy the defense reaction time.

Caveira.description: Allows Caveira to move without making sound, enabling her to approach enemies undetected for interrogations.
Caveira.usageNote: Best used off-objective for flanking and roaming rather than anchoring near the site itself.

Thermite.description: Burns through reinforced walls and other surfaces with an intense thermite reaction to create breaches for the attacking team.
Thermite.usageNote: Use on reinforced walls near the objective, ideally after clearing likely anti-breach gadgets like Bandit or Kaid devices.

Osa.description: A transparent, bulletproof shield that can be carried or deployed on floors and window frames to provide protective cover during tactical operations.
Osa.usageNote: Deploy in open lanes or doorways to create safe lines of advance and shared cover for the attacking team.

Zero.description: Deploys special cameras that lodge into surfaces to provide surveillance of multiple angles and can fire laser shots to destroy defender equipment.
Zero.usageNote: Use pre-round or early in the round to scout the objective and disable defender gadgets from a distance.

Hibana.description: A 40mm caliber launcher that fires explosive pellets which can be detonated simultaneously from a distance to create new lines of sight or pathways through obstacles.
Hibana.usageNote: Use on reinforced walls to open precise sightlines or murder holes rather than full breaches.

Ace.description: Uses hydraulic pressure to breach through reinforced and breakable surfaces, creating rectangular openings that can combine to form larger holes for operator passage.
Ace.usageNote: Use on reinforced walls near the objective to create a controlled breach point for the attacking team's entry.

Grim.description: Fires a projectile that releases a swarm of tracking bots to reveal opponent locations when they pass through it.
Grim.usageNote: Deploy in likely defender rotation paths or roam areas to track movement ahead of a push.

Ying.description: A mercury and magnesium-based explosive device that releases a cluster of flash charges to disorient enemies, deployable as an anchored gadget or thrown grenade.
Ying.usageNote: Throw or deploy near the objective entry point to disorient defenders immediately before a push.

Ash.description: Modified breaching rounds fired from a launcher that allow ranged soft breaching, capable of destroying defensive utilities like barricades, bulletproof cameras, and barbed wire.
Ash.usageNote: Use at range to quickly clear soft barricades, cameras, or utility without needing to approach the objective directly.

Zofia.description: An electronically triggered projectile launcher that fires concussion rounds delivering a shockwave that causes disorientation, or impact ammunition for breaching purposes.
Zofia.usageNote: Use concussion rounds to disorient defenders near the objective before a push, or impact rounds to clear soft cover.

Striker.description: Striker has no distinctive signature gadget; the official page states he is the Attack operator with access to all secondary gadgets for Attack operators (e.g. Breach Charge, Claymore, Frag Grenade, Hard Breach Charge, Smoke Grenade, Stun Grenade, Impact EMP Grenade).
Striker.usageNote: Loadout flexibility is the value here; choose secondary gadgets to fill whatever gap the rest of the attacking team's kit leaves open.

Amaru.description: A grappling hook device that lets Amaru quickly ascend to ledges and windows, launching herself upward with a special entry.
Amaru.usageNote: Use to open unconventional vertical entry points onto the objective floor, catching defenders off guard from unexpected angles.

Buck.description: An under-barrel mounted 12-gauge shotgun attachment with a four round capacity, used alongside his primary assault rifle to punch holes through soft surfaces.
Buck.usageNote: Use to create small murder holes or sightlines into the objective from unreinforced walls and floors/ceilings.

Capitão.description: A crossbow that fires glass bolts which shatter on impact, releasing a gas that ignites the surrounding air to deny an area or damage defenders.
Capitão.usageNote: Fire through soft surfaces or into rooms to deny defender positions or set up follow-up pushes without exposing yourself directly.

Castle.description: Reinforced barricades that Castle can deploy over doors and windows to block off entry points more durably than standard barricades.
Castle.usageNote: Use on secondary entry points to fully seal off flanks, forcing attackers toward chokepoints the defending team can cover.

Clash.description: A riot shield that can anchor to the ground and remotely taser nearby enemies to slow their movement while Clash roams freely to reposition.
Clash.usageNote: Use to hold and slow down a push at a chokepoint while buying time for teammates to rotate or reinforce elsewhere.

Doc.description: A handheld, trigger-operated pistol that delivers a dose of epinephrine in saline solution, healing and reviving allies or self-healing.
Doc.usageNote: Keep it available to heal teammates without needing them to retreat, or to self-heal while holding an anchor position.

Dokkaebi.description: Malware uploaded to enemy phones that blocks their observation tools and can be detonated to damage the carrying defender and nearby operators with fire.
Dokkaebi.usageNote: Use after defender phones are hacked to deny re-use of cameras and gadgets, ideally timed with a coordinated push.

Echo.description: A signature drone equipped with an acoustic hailing device that fires ultrasonic bursts to disorient any attackers within its effect radius.
Echo.usageNote: Fly to intel positions or chokepoints to disrupt pushes and gather information, retreating to safety before it can be destroyed.

Fenrir.description: Throwable mines that stick to walls and, when triggered, afflict nearby attackers with a fear effect that distorts their vision and audio.
Fenrir.usageNote: Place in likely attacker approach paths near the objective to disorient pushes before they can clear the site.

Gridlock.description: A deployable device that releases a hexagonal cluster of caltrop-like spikes covering the ground, which can replicate and spread over a wider area.
Gridlock.usageNote: Deploy behind you to deny flank routes and rotations after pushing into the objective, slowing down defender counter-attacks.

Jäger.description: A automated unit that detects and destroys incoming projectiles, such as grenades, within its line of sight before they can land.
Jäger.usageNote: Place facing common grenade-throw angles into the objective to protect the site from thrown attacker utility.

Lion.description: A drone that scans an area to detect any movement, briefly revealing the location of moving defenders across the map during its scan.
Lion.usageNote: Time the scan with a coordinated team push so defenders are forced to choose between standing still or being revealed.

Maestro.description: A remote-controlled, bulletproof turret that fires a high-energy laser and provides overwatch through a camera, retaining vision even through smoke.
Maestro.usageNote: Place with an angle on a key approach or hallway, since the turret is only vulnerable while its shutter is open to fire.

Nøkk.description: A glove attachment that, when activated, reduces her ambient noise output and hides her from enemy observation tools like cameras and drones.
Nøkk.usageNote: Use to flank quietly or approach cameras and drones undetected, then use the window for a surprise entry or gadget disable.

Nomad.description: A rifle attachment that launches Airjab grenades which stick to a surface and detonate a repulsion blast to knock back and disorient enemies in range.
Nomad.usageNote: Use to deny a window or door defenders might use to counter-push, or to push back a defender aggressively holding an angle.

Sens.description: A throwable device that rolls along the ground and drops small projectors, creating a visual screen along its path that blocks lines of sight while still allowing physical passage.
Sens.usageNote: Use to cut off a defender's sightline into a room or lane so the team can cross or push through safely.

Sledge.description: A tactical hammer that lets Sledge quickly smash through non-reinforced walls, floors, and ceilings to create openings or sightlines.
Sledge.usageNote: Use to make fast, quiet-ish openings into soft walls for surprise angles or entry points the defense may not expect.

Solid Snake.description: A handheld device with a minimap that marks nearby hostiles, including a precision mode to pinpoint threats at the cost of alerting them that they've been detected.
Solid Snake.usageNote: Use to check nearby corners and rooms for enemy presence before pushing, being mindful that precision mode can give away your own position.

Solis.description: A detection apparatus that lets Solis mark and ping the location of opponents' electronic devices, revealing gadgets and nearby attackers through walls.
Solis.usageNote: Use to scan likely gadget-heavy approach routes and reveal attacker positions before they reach the objective.

Vigil.description: A backpack device using Diminished Reality technology that scans surrounding electronic devices and removes Vigil's image from any cameras and drones in view.
Vigil.usageNote: Activate before crossing drone or camera sightlines while roaming or repositioning to avoid revealing your location to attackers.

Wamai.description: A thrown, adhesive gadget that sticks to a surface and attracts nearby enemy projectiles to its position, self-destructing to detonate them harmlessly.
Wamai.usageNote: Place near likely grenade or gadget throw angles into the objective to neutralize incoming projectile utility before it lands.

Warden.description: Smart glasses that grant thermal vision to see through smoke while stationary, and can also prevent or cleanse flash-grenade blindness.
Warden.usageNote: Activate while holding an angle through smoke-filled areas, but be aware the thermal effect only works while standing still.

Fuze.description: A charge that propels a group of explosive cluster grenades through a soft breach surface, dispersing across a targeted area to damage enemies and destroy gadgets.
Fuze.usageNote: Fire into rooms with likely defender anchors or gadget clusters to clear the space before the team pushes in.

Montagne.description: A reinforced alloy and Kevlar riot shield that protects against small-arms fire and close-quarters attacks, and can collapse to half its size for increased mobility.
Montagne.usageNote: Lead a push through an exposed lane or doorway to soak fire and create safe cover for teammates following behind.
